criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified

criteria_results.title: Scienter Distinction Between Section 206(1) and 206(2) Flagged
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies the scienter distinction between Section 206(1) and Section 206(2) in the 'Discrepancies and Defense Issues' section. criteria_results.title: ISSUE_006: Statute of Limitations Concern Under 28 U.S.C. Â§ 2462
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does raise the five-year statute of limitations under 28 U.S.C. § 2462 in the 'Discrepancies and Defense Issues' section, noting that 'Any conduct occurring more than five years before the action is commenced may be time-barred for penalty purposes.' However, the criterion specifically requires the output to identify that early 2021 conduct may be approaching or past the five-year limitations period given the Review Period starts January 1, 2021, and the referral was made October 18/24, 2024 (approximately 3 years and 9-10 months). The agent also incorrectly states 'disgorgement runs on a separate clock,' which conflicts with Kokesh v. SEC (which the criterion specifically references and which held disgorgement is subject to § 2462). The criterion requires identification of the specific temporal risk for early 2021 conduct given the October 2024 referral date, which the agent fails to provide.
